testcase.name: tests.functional.test_coder_capabilities
testcase.error: ImportError while importing test module 'C:\Users\Lyndz\Downloads\HyperFocus-IDE-BROski-v1\tests\functional\test_coder_capabilities.py'. Hint: make sure your test modules/packages have valid Python names. Traceback: C:\Python313\Lib\importlib\__init__.py:88: in import_module return _bootstrap._gcd_import(name[level:], package, level) tests\functional\test_coder_capabilities.py:7: in <module> from app.core.config import get_settings E ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'app.core.config'

testcase.name: test_agents_status
testcase.failure:
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_agents_status(): """Test that all agents are reachable""" async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: response = await client.get(f"{ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/agents/status") assert response.status_code == 200 agents = response.json()
# Should have all 8 agents assert len(agents) == 8
# Check critical agents are online agent_names = [a["agent"] for a in agents] > assert "project-strategist" in str(agent_names) E assert 'project-strategist' in "['project_strategist', 'frontend_specialist', 'backend_specialist', 'database_architect', 'qa_engineer', 'devops_engineer', 'security_engineer', 'system_architect']" E + where "['project_strategist', 'frontend_specialist', 'backend_specialist', 'database_architect', 'qa_engineer', 'devops_engineer', 'security_engineer', 'system_architect']" = str(['project_strategist', 'frontend_specialist', 'backend_specialist', 'database_architect', 'qa_engineer', 'devops_engineer', ...])
tests\test_agent_crew.py:33: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_agent_direct_execution
testcase.failure:
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_agent_direct_execution(): """Test direct agent execution""" async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client: response = await client.post( f"{ORCHESTRATOR_URL}/agent/frontend-specialist/execute", json={ "agent": "frontend-specialist", "message": "Create a button component", "context": {} } ) # May fail if agent is busy, but should not return 404 > assert response.status_code in [200, 503] E assert 404 in [200, 503] E + where 404 = <Response [404 Not Found]>.status_code
tests\test_agent_crew.py:82: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_websocket_connection

failure.message: Failed: WebSocket connection failed: server rejected WebSocket connection: HTTP 200

testcase.failure:
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_websocket_connection(): """ Verify that we can establish a WebSocket connection and receive the initial connection success message/state. """ try: > async with websockets.connect(WS_URL) as websocket:
tests\integration\test_websocket_integration.py:19: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ .venv\Lib\site-packages\websockets\asyncio\client.py:590: in __aenter__ return await self .venv\Lib\site-packages\websockets\asyncio\client.py:546: in __await_impl__ await self.connection.handshake( .venv\Lib\site-packages\websockets\asyncio\client.py:115: in handshake raise self.protocol.handshake_exc .venv\Lib\site-packages\websockets\client.py:327: in parse self.process_response(response) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <websockets.client.ClientProtocol object at 0x000002100EC09E50> response = Response(status_code=200, reason_phrase='OK', headers=Headers([('Date', 'Tue, 24 Feb 2026 14:09:00 GMT'), ('Content-Ty...rue , "../", true ));\n drawImages( null );\n </script>\n </body>\n</html>\n', _exception=InvalidStatus(...))
def process_response(self, response: Response) -> None: """ Check a handshake response.
Args: request: WebSocket handshake response received from the server.
Raises: InvalidHandshake: If the handshake response is invalid.
"""
if response.status_code != 101: > raise InvalidStatus(response) E websockets.exceptions.InvalidStatus: server rejected WebSocket connection: HTTP 200
.venv\Lib\site-packages\websockets\client.py:144: InvalidStatus
During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_websocket_connection(): """ Verify that we can establish a WebSocket connection and receive the initial connection success message/state. """ try: async with websockets.connect(WS_URL) as websocket: # Send a ping to verify bidirectional communication await websocket.send("ping") response = await websocket.recv() assert response == "pong" except Exception as e: > pytest.fail(f"WebSocket connection failed: {e}") E Failed: WebSocket connection failed: server rejected WebSocket connection: HTTP 200
tests\integration\test_websocket_integration.py:25: Failed

testcase.name: test_realtime_task_update

failure.message: websockets.exceptions.InvalidStatus: server rejected WebSocket connection: HTTP 200

testcase.failure:
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_realtime_task_update(): """ Verify that creating a task via REST API triggers a real-time notification on the WebSocket. """ task_payload = { "task": "Integration Test Task", "context": {"env": "test"}, "priority": "high" }
> async with websockets.connect(WS_URL) as websocket:

def process_response(self, response: Response) -> None: """ Check a handshake response.
Args: request: WebSocket handshake response received from the server.
Raises: InvalidHandshake: If the handshake response is invalid.

if response.status_code != 101: > raise InvalidStatus(response) E websockets.exceptions.InvalidStatus: server rejected WebSocket connection: HTTP 200

failure.message: websockets.exceptions.InvalidStatus: server rejected WebSocket connection: HTTP 200

testcase.failure:
@pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_workflow_broadcast(): """ Verify that starting a workflow broadcasts an event. """ workflow_payload = { "workflow_type": "feature", "description": "Test Workflow Broadcast", "requirements": {} }
> async with websockets.connect(WS_URL) as websocket:
